name: ASIP Self-Evolution Mechanism
description: Evaluates whether Forgewright's ASIP self-evolution mechanism triggers correctly: research gate enforcement, lesson persistence, session→skill migration, and cross-feedback loop.

testCases.name: Init script bootstraps all baseline files
testCases.expected_behavior: All infrastructure files present and scripts are executable
success_criteria: asip-state.json exists and is valid JSON
asip-metrics.json exists and is valid JSON
status output shows ALL SYSTEMS OK

testCases.name: Deterministic check: plan with valid files passes
testCases.expected_behavior: Script detects the file exists and all referenced paths are valid
success_criteria: Exit code 0 (PASS) or 2 (WARNINGS — acceptable for protocol docs)
Output mentions 'All referenced files exist' or similar

testCases.name: Deterministic check: plan with missing files reports failures
testCases.prompt:
Run: echo '# Plan

1. Edit skills/nonexistent-skill/SKILL.md
2. Modify scripts/missing-script.sh' | bash scripts/asip-deterministic-check.sh /dev/stdin
testCases.expected_behavior: Script detects non-existent files and reports FAIL
success_criteria: At least one [FAIL] or [WARN] for the missing files

testCases.name: Deterministic check: testability passes when test commands exist
testCases.prompt:
Run: echo '# Plan

1. Write tests using pytest
2. Run: pytest tests/
3. Acceptance: pytest exit code = 0' | bash scripts/asip-deterministic-check.sh /dev/stdin
testCases.expected_behavior: Script detects test commands and acceptance criteria references

testCases.name: Deterministic check: testability fails when no test commands
testCases.prompt:
Run: echo '# Plan

1. Implement feature
2. Done' | bash scripts/asip-deterministic-check.sh /dev/stdin
testCases.expected_behavior: Script detects missing test commands and issues a warning
success_criteria: Output contains warning about 'No specific test commands'
Output contains warning about 'No acceptance criteria'

testCases.name: Lesson migrator: status shows empty state initially
testCases.expected_behavior: Shows migration state with zero migrated entries

testCases.name: Lesson migrator: migrate with no new entries is a no-op
testCases.expected_behavior: Reports no new lessons to migrate, exits cleanly
success_criteria: Output says 'No new plan lessons' or 'No new execution lessons'

testCases.name: Lesson migrator: appends Planning Improvements section if missing
testCases.prompt: Simulate adding a lesson entry to plan-lessons.md, then run migrate
testCases.expected_behavior: Lesson migrator finds the entry and appends to target skill's Planning Improvements
success_criteria: Migration state updates with migratedPlanEntries count
No duplicate entries on second run (idempotent)
testCases.setup: Append test entry to .forgewright/plan-lessons.md, record state

testCases.name: Session tracker: init and status work
testCases.prompt: Run: bash scripts/forgewright-session-tracker.sh init && bash scripts/forgewright-session-tracker.sh status
testCases.expected_behavior: Initializes tracking state and shows zero failures

testCases.name: Session tracker: records plan failure and triggers gate at threshold
testCases.prompt: Run: bash scripts/forgewright-session-tracker.sh plan 7.5 && bash scripts/forgewright-session-tracker.sh plan 7.0 && bash scripts/forgewright-session-tracker.sh check

testCases.name: Init script: update-metrics works
testCases.expected_behavior: Increments lessonsLearned by 3 for software-engineer skill
success_criteria: JSON is still valid after update

testCases.name: Init script: self-verify passes
testCases.expected_behavior: All scripts have valid shell syntax

testCases.name: Cross-feedback: execution lesson generates planning stub
testCases.prompt: Add an execution lesson with 'Planning Review Trigger' fields to execution-lessons.md, run migrate
testCases.expected_behavior: Lesson migrator detects the planning review trigger and generates a stub in plan-lessons.md
success_criteria: execution-lessons.md entry with planning gap is migrated
A new entry appears in plan-lessons.md referencing the execution lesson
The stub contains the planning gap description
testCases.setup: Append test execution lesson with planning gap to .forgewright/execution-lessons.md
testCases.teardown: Remove test entries from both lesson files

testCases.name: Init script: idempotent (running twice doesn't corrupt state)
testCases.expected_behavior: Second run reports 'already exists' for all files, state is unchanged
success_criteria: All files report 'already exists' on second run
asip-state.json and asip-metrics.json are still valid JSON
